assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and a corresponding receivable/payable has been recognised, so the item is a recognised intragroup monetary item.
The receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign currency risk between group entities with different functional currencies.
The resulting foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and could affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup receivable can be a hedged item in a fair value hedge for foreign currency risk if that risk affects consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The foreign currency exposure on settlement of the recognised intragroup monetary item can also be considered under the cash flow hedge model.
